[[co:commons:Category:Udo-jingu]][[de:Udo-Schrein]][[en:Udo Jingū]][[fr:Udo-jingū]][[ja:鵜戸神宮]][[ko:우도 신궁]][[zh-yue:鵜戶神宮]][[zh:鵜戶神宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Udo-jingu]][[de:Udo-Schrein]][[en:Udo Jingū]][[fr:Udo-jingū]][[ja:鵜戸神宮]][[ko:우도 신궁]][[zh-yue:鵜戶神宮]][[zh:鵜戶神宮]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Udo-jingu]][[de:Udo-Schrein]][[en:Udo Jingū]][[fr:Udo-jingū]][[ja:鵜戸神宮]][[ko:우도 신궁]][[zh-yue:鵜戶神宮]][[zh:鵜戶神宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Miyazaki Prefecture, Japan}} {{one source|date=November 2021}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Udo-jingū
鵜戸神宮 | image = Udo-jingu Main hall 001.jpg | caption = The ''[[honden]]'' (main shrine) | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|31|39|01|N|131|28|00|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = [[Ugayafukiaezu]]
[[Amaterasu]]
{{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Ame-no-oshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}
[[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]]
[[Hoori]]
{{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = 3232, Miyaura, {{ill|Nichinan, Miyazaki|en|Nichinan, Miyazaki|WD=Q860669|lt=Nichinan|1=Nichinan, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}} 887-0101 | website = {{URL|http://www.udojingu.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Udo-jingū'''|鵜戸神宮|}} is a Shinto shrine in {{ill|Nichinan, Miyazaki|en|Nichinan, Miyazaki|WD=Q860669|lt=Nichinan|1=Nichinan, Miyazaki}}, {{ill|Miyazaki Prefecture|en|Miyazaki Prefecture|WD=Q130300|lt=Miyazaki Prefecture|1=Miyazaki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, south of {{ill|Aoshima, Miyazaki|en|Aoshima, Miyazaki|WD=Q4778779|lt=Aoshima|1=Aoshima, Miyazaki}}. It is the mythical birthplace of {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}'s father [[Ugayafukiaezu]].{{Cite book|last=Picken|first=Stuart D. B.|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=xlG3F6gSm9UC&dq=Udo-jing%C5%AB&pg=PA307|title=Historical Dictionary of Shinto|date=2010-12-28|publisher=Scarecrow Press|isbn=978-0-8108-7372-8|pages=307|language=en}} According to shrine legends, it is the place where the sea goddess [[:en:Toyotamahime|Toyotamahime]], the mother of said Ugayafukiaezu, built a birth-hut from the feathers of a {{ill|Cormorant|en|Cormorant|WD=Q3901247|lt=Cormorant|1=Cormorant}}. Other gods venerated here are Yamasachihiko (alias Hohodemi alias [[Hoori]], Jinmu's grandfather), [[Amaterasu]], {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|en|Ame-no-oshihomimi|WD=Q462555|lt=Amenooshihomimi|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi}}, [[Ninigi-no-Mikoto]], and Emperor Jimmu. While the original myth includes a tragic divorce of Ugayafukiaezu's parents, the shrine is popular with young couples hoping for easy childbirth and a happy marriage. == Local characteristics == Udo shrine is in a cave in the side of the cliff, near the Nichinan coast of Miyazaki. The ''honden'', or main shrine, is in a cavern with a view of the ocean. In the cave is the ''ochichi iwa'', or "breast stone," a dripping stone which is said to have fed the kami Ugayafukiaezu, father of the first emperor of Japan, when his mother returned to the sea. The shrine sells a candy made from the water of this stone, ''mizuame'', a kind of taffy.{{cite book|last1=Dougill|first1=John|last2=Cali|first2=Joseph|title=Shinto shrines : a guide to the sacred sites of Japan's ancient religion|date=2013|publisher=University of Hawaiʻi Press|location=Honolulu|isbn=9780824837136|url=http://muse.jhu.edu/books/9780824837754}}{{subscription required|via=[https://muse.jhu.edu/ Project MUSE]}} In the slight distance of the cavern is a rocky island with two peaks. Attendees can purchase small clay balls and attempt to toss them between the peaks for good luck. == References == {{Commons Category|Udo-jingu|Udo-jingū}} {{Reflist}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Udo Jingū]] {{Commons category|Udo-jingu}} [[de:Udo-Schrein]] [[fr:Udo-jingū]] [[ja:鵜戸神宮]] [[ko:우도 신궁]] [[zh:鵜戶神宮]] [[zh-yue:鵜戶神宮]] {{wikidata link|Q704430}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Kanpei Taisha]] [[Category:Miyazaki prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Nichinan, Miyazaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyazaki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
